$comment: Race results desk pack (prefix rr.) - everyday result coverage grown past 010-core: era-voiced generics for raceWon/podiumFinish/pointsFinish/midfieldResult/overperformed/underperformed (1960s-70s formal report, 1980s-90s back page, 2000s-10s digital, smgp arcade board), plus situational specials core lacks (season-closing win, opener win, back-to-back, rival-beaten, wet podium, giant-killing overdelivery, flat finale). Read-side only: editing this file changes display, never replay.

templates.headline: Victory[[?venue: at {venue}]] for {subject}
templates.deck: The honours of the day[[?round: in round {round}]] go to {subject}
templates.summary: {subject} won[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?round: in round {round}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} It is the pleasant duty of this correspondent to report a victory for {subject}[[?team: , with the {team} entry running faultlessly from flag to flag]]. {pool:rrColor}
sections.context: [[?expected: The form book had suggested {ord:expected}; the driver evidently had not read it.]] [[?quali: From {ord:quali} on the grid, the win was made on the road, in the old style.]] [[?champPosition: The championship account now stands at {ord:champPosition}.]]

templates.headline: No arguments: {subject} takes the big one[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: Fastest when it counted[[?round: in round {round}]]
templates.summary: {subject} won[[?round: round {round}]][[?venue: at {venue}]] and made it look like a plan.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Forget the subplots. {subject} hit the front, stayed there when it mattered, and banked the points everyone else spent the weekend talking about. {pool:rrColor}

templates.headline: {subject} wins[[?venue: at {venue}]] - as it happened
templates.deck: Race report[[?round: , round {round}]]: control from lights to flag
templates.summary: {subject} converted the whole weekend into victory[[?venue: at {venue}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The win looked inevitable from about half distance, which is exactly how {subject} wanted it to look. The gaps were managed, the tyres lasted, and the pit wall's tone never changed. {pool:rrColor}
sections.stats: {pool:rrStatLine} [[?quali: The weekend arc: {ord:quali} on Saturday, first on Sunday.]]
sections.next: The next round's storylines write themselves now[[?champPosition: - {ord:champPosition} in the table and the momentum metrics all pointing one way]].

templates.headline: {subject} TAKES THE CHECKER. FLAWLESS.
templates.summary: {subject} won[[?round: round {round}]] and the big board knows it.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} GREEN LIGHTS. FULL SEND. {subject} DROVE THE PERFECT LINE[[?venue: AROUND {venue}]] AND THE FIELD SAW NOTHING BUT SLIPSTREAM. {pool:rrColor}
sections.impact: [[?team: {team} PIT CREW: MAXIMUM COMBO. ]]THE LADDER MOVES. THE CROWD KNOWS THE NAME.

templates.headline: {subject} signs off the {year} season with victory
templates.deck: The final round[[?venue: at {venue}]] goes to {subject}
templates.summary: {subject} won the {year} season finale[[?venue: at {venue}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Seasons are remembered by how they end, and {year} ends with {subject} on the top step[[?venue: at {venue}]]. The last flag of the year fell on a win.
sections.context: Final-round wins carry their own currency: they are the result everyone replays over the winter, the note a season holds until the next one begins. Whatever else {year} was, its closing line now belongs to {subject}.
sections.close: The transporters pack for the last time this year, and one garage packs happy.

templates.headline: First blood of {year}: {subject} wins the opener
templates.deck: The new season has an early author
templates.summary: {subject} won the opening round of the {year} season[[?venue: at {venue}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} A winter of promises met its first stopwatch today, and {subject} answered loudest: victory in the {year} season opener[[?venue: at {venue}]].
sections.context: Openers set tables and tones. Nobody wins a championship in the first round, but somebody always leaves it holding the early script[[?team: , and this year the script sits in {team's} garage]].

templates.headline: No let-up: {subject} wins again
templates.deck: [[?streak: That is {streak} in a row now]]
templates.summary: {subject} won again[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?streak: - {ord:streak} straight]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The paddock waited for the reply, and it came from the same garage as last time: {subject} won again[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?streak: , a {ord:streak} victory in succession]], and the rest of the field is officially on notice.
sections.context: One win can be a weekend. Two in a row is a direction. Teams do not stumble into consecutive victories - they build them, and the building is the frightening part for everyone else.

templates.headline: {subject} wins the one that mattered[[?rival: - {rival} beaten]]
templates.deck: A victory with a name attached
templates.summary: {subject} won[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?rival: with {rival} in the mirrors]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Some wins are worth more than the points[[?rival: , and beating {rival} to the flag is one of them]]. {subject} took the victory[[?venue: at {venue}]] in a straight fight, the kind both camps will remember.
sections.rivalry: [[?rival: {rival} will call it one round of a long argument, and that is exactly what it is - but the scoreboard in this rivalry moved today, and it moved {player's} way. ]]{pool:paddockBuzz}

templates.headline: {subject} among the honours[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: A rostrum finish[[?round: in round {round}]] of the {year} season
templates.summary: {subject} took a place on the rostrum[[?position: with {ord:position}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} {subject} drove a measured race into the honours[[?position: , classified {ord:position} at the flag,]] and collected the sort of result upon which sound seasons are built. {pool:rrColor}
sections.context: [[?team: The {team} crew will take quiet satisfaction from the day's work.]] [[?champPosition: The championship table now shows {ord:champPosition}.]]

templates.headline: Champagne again: {subject} on the box[[?position: in {ord:position}]]
templates.summary: {subject} finished on the podium[[?venue: at {venue}]] - silverware and points.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} {subject} muscled onto the podium[[?venue: at {venue}]] and let the champagne do the talking. No gifts, no luck - just a driver taking what the race offered and not a scrap less. {pool:rrColor}

templates.headline: Podium delivered: {subject}[[?position: takes {ord:position}]][[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: The strategy paid off in full[[?round: in round {round}]]
templates.summary: {subject} finished on the podium[[?position: in {ord:position}]][[?venue: at {venue}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Podiums like this are engineered across every session of the weekend, and {subject} closed the deal on Sunday[[?position: with {ord:position}]]. Track position, tyre life, clean air at the right moments - the whole checklist, ticked. {pool:rrColor}
sections.stats: {pool:rrStatLine} [[?expected: The pre-race model said {ord:expected}. The driver had other data.]]

templates.headline: {subject} HITS THE PODIUM. CONFETTI EVERYWHERE.
templates.deck: TOP THREE[[?venue: AT {venue}]]. THE LADDER APPROVES.
templates.summary: {subject} landed on the podium[[?position: in P{position}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} {subject} CARVED THROUGH THE PACK AND PLANTED THE CAR ON THE ROSTRUM[[?position: - P{position} IN LIGHTS TEN FEET TALL]]. {pool:rrColor}
sections.impact: PODIUM POINTS BANKED. MOMENTUM METER: RISING. [[?team: {team} GARAGE IS LOUD TONIGHT.]]

templates.headline: Rain-tamer: {subject} on the podium in the wet
templates.deck: A soaked afternoon[[?venue: at {venue}]] rewards the brave
templates.summary: {subject} took a podium in the rain[[?position: with {ord:position}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The rain turned the circuit into a lottery for most of the field. {subject} declined the ticket, kept the car pointed the right way, and drove onto the podium[[?position: in {ord:position}]] while others aquaplaned out of the story.
sections.context: Wet podiums are scouting reports written in public: car control, judgment, nerve, all measured under the worst conditions the sport can offer. The paddock files these results away and remembers them at contract time.

templates.headline: Points for {subject}[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.summary: {subject} added to the season's account[[?position: with {ord:position}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} {subject} brought the car home[[?position: in {ord:position}]] and entered points in the ledger - unglamorous, essential work of the kind championships are quietly assembled from.
sections.context: [[?champPosition: The table stands at {ord:champPosition} with the season in motion.]] {pool:champChatter}
sections.close: The machinery is loaded; the season moves to its next appointment.

templates.headline: {subject} cashes in[[?position: with {ord:position}]]
templates.summary: Points in the bank for {subject}[[?round: in round {round}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} No fireworks, no drama, no complaints: {subject} kept it clean, kept it quick, and left with points while half the paddock left with excuses. {pool:rrColor}
sections.stats: {pool:rrStatLine} [[?champPosition: The table says {ord:champPosition}. The table is the only critic that counts.]]

templates.headline: {subject} BANKS THE POINTS. THE COUNTER TICKS UP.
templates.summary: Points on the board for {subject}[[?position: from P{position}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} NOT EVERY RACE IS A HIGHLIGHT REEL. {subject} TOOK THE POINTS ON OFFER[[?position: FROM P{position}]] AND FED THE SEASON TOTAL. THE LADDER COUNTS EVERYTHING.
sections.impact: EVERY POINT FEEDS THE LADDER. THE LADDER FEEDS THE LEGEND.

templates.headline: A workmanlike day for {subject}
templates.summary: {subject} finished[[?position: {ord:position}]] - a day of honest motoring, no more.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Not every Sunday furnishes a tale for the grandchildren. {subject} circulated[[?position: to {ord:position}]], the machinery held together, and the season's education continued by a page or two.

templates.headline: Nothing doing: {subject} stuck in the pack
templates.summary: {subject} came home[[?position: {ord:position}]] - one of those Sundays.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Some races you win, some you survive, and some just happen around you. {subject} spent the afternoon in traffic[[?position: and came out of it {ord:position}]], with nothing to show but mileage and a tyre bill.
sections.context: The garage will not panic over a day like this. They will, however, remember it at the next upgrade meeting.

templates.headline: {subject} anonymous in the midfield[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.summary: {subject} finished[[?position: {ord:position},]] out of the points and out of the broadcast.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The world feed barely found {subject} all afternoon. The car ran[[?position: to {ord:position}]], the strategy options never opened, and the race simply expired. {pool:rrColor}
sections.next: Debrief question of the week: was that the car's ceiling, or the weekend's?

templates.headline: MID-PACK FINISH. THE BOARD BARELY BLINKS.
templates.summary: {subject} ran the race[[?position: to P{position}]]. No fireworks.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} NOT EVERY ROUND IS A BOSS STAGE. {subject} TOOK THE FLAG[[?position: IN P{position}]], POCKETED THE EXPERIENCE, AND LIVED TO CLIMB ANOTHER DAY.

templates.headline: Where did THAT come from? {subject} stuns the pit lane
templates.deck: Analysis: the result the form book refused to print
templates.summary: {subject} finished far above the car's station[[?position: in {ord:position}]].

sections.lead: The form book said one thing[[?expected: - {ord:expected}, no arguments -]] and {subject} spent Sunday afternoon tearing the page out[[?position: , hauling the thing to {ord:position}]]. {pool:rrColor}
sections.stats: {pool:rrStatLine} Overdeliveries get noticed in every garage on the pit lane, especially the ones with faster cars and slower drivers.

templates.headline: Outlier alert: {subject} beats the model[[?position: with {ord:position}]]
templates.deck: The projection had one number. The stopwatch had another.
templates.summary: {subject} outran the car's pace[[?expected: - expected {ord:expected}]][[?position: , delivered {ord:position}]].

sections.lead: Every team runs predictive models, and every model had the same blind spot this weekend: the driver. {subject} finished[[?position: {ord:position}]][[?expected: against a projected {ord:expected}]], and the delta is all driver.
sections.next: Watch the engineers' body language next weekend: results like this reset what a team believes its package can do.

templates.headline: Giant-killing: {subject} drags the impossible out of the car
templates.deck: The overdelivery of the season[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.summary: {subject} beat the car's true pace by the best part of a grid[[?expected: - {ord:expected} expected]][[?position: , {ord:position} delivered]].

sections.lead: There are good drives, there are great drives, and then there are days when a driver simply refuses the equipment's verdict. {subject} produced one of those[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?position: , carrying the car to {ord:position}]][[?expected: when every honest measure said {ord:expected}]].
sections.impact: Results like this change careers. Team managers with faster cars watch them twice; engineers pull the data to check it is real; and somewhere up the pit lane, a contract conversation just changed its tone.

templates.headline: Flat as a Monday: {subject} goes missing
templates.deck: Analysis: the pace was there. The result was not.
templates.summary: {subject} fell short of the car[[?position: with {ord:position}]][[?expected: against an expected {ord:expected}]].

sections.lead: The car turned up. The stopwatch says the driver did not[[?position: - {ord:position} at the flag]][[?expected: from machinery worth {ord:expected}]]. Days like this cost more than points; they cost the benefit of the doubt.
sections.context: The garage kept its counsel afterwards, which is its own kind of verdict.

templates.headline: Under the line: {subject} leaves points on the table
templates.summary: {subject} finished below the car's pace[[?expected: - projected {ord:expected}]][[?position: , classified {ord:position}]].

sections.lead: The overlays make uncomfortable viewing: the pace was in the car[[?expected: for {ord:expected} or better]], and the result sheet[[?position: - {ord:position} -]] says otherwise. Somewhere between Saturday night and the chequered flag, a weekend leaked away.
sections.stats: {pool:rrStatLine} The debrief will be data-driven, thorough, and no fun at all.

templates.headline: A season ends with a shrug: {subject} fades in the finale
templates.summary: {subject} closed the {year} season below expectations[[?position: in {ord:position}]].

sections.lead: Final rounds are for statements, and {subject} left this one unsigned[[?position: , drifting home {ord:position}]][[?expected: where the car was good for {ord:expected}]]. The season's last impression is a flat one, and last impressions winter badly.
sections.context: Every driver knows the arithmetic of memory: the paddock forgets a quiet May, never a quiet finale. There are months now to think about that.

1960s: The public address crackled over a full house, and the crowd pressed ten deep at the fences.
Straw bales, rope lines, and a paddock you could walk through with a programme and a pencil.
The smell of castor oil hung over the pits all afternoon.
1980s: Turbo shriek, sponsor caps, and a pit lane shouting arithmetic at itself.
The grandstands were a wall of flags, and none of them neutral.
Money and noise everywhere - the sport at full volume.
2010s: The timing screens told the story before the commentary could catch up.
The paddock's phones were out before the cars reached parc ferme.
Every sector time hit the feed in real time, and the feed did not blink.
smgp: COIN-BRIGHT SKIES. PERFECT RACING WEATHER. MAXIMUM CROWD.
THE BIG BOARD FLASHED EVERY LAP OF IT.
default: The grandstands had their money's worth long before the flag.
It was one of those afternoons the calendar circles for you.
The weekend gave nothing away for free.

1960s: The timekeepers' sheets will be studied by lamplight tonight.
The record of it is entered in ink[[?position: : {ord:position} at the fall of the flag]].
2010s: The data engineers will strip this one back to the microsecond.
The sector traces all tell the same story[[?position: : {ord:position}, earned the hard way]].
smgp: THE NUMBERS DO NOT LIE. THE BOARD NEVER DOES.
[[?position: P{position} GOES UP IN LIGHTS. ]]THE LADDER RECALCULATES.
default: The stopwatch keeps the only honest ledger in this sport[[?position: , and today it wrote {ord:position}]].
Strip away the noise and the numbers remain[[?quali: : {ord:quali} on Saturday]][[?position: , {ord:position} on Sunday]].
Statisticians live for weekends like this one.

1970s: Filed from a paddock loud with sponsor money[[?venue: at {venue}]].
1990s: Faxed from the paddock[[?venue: at {venue}]] before the presses rolled.
2000s: Filed from the media centre[[?venue: at {venue}]], laptops still warm.

1980s: Somebody in that garage is buying tonight.
Back page sorted. Front page pending.
2010s: The highlights package will not do it justice.
Trending for all the right reasons.

1990s: The motorhome blinds came down early.
Monday's debrief already looms, and nobody is looking forward to it.
